description_long: This is a tall, narrow-necked terracotta vase with a bulbous body and a slightly flared rim. The surface is adorned with intricate black painted motifs, including stylized birds, spirals, and geometric patterns, reminiscent of ancient or traditional pottery styles. The reddish-brown clay body is contrasted by the deep black decorative elements, which wrap around the entire circumference of the vase. The neck features horizontal black bands, while the main body displays the most elaborate artwork, with the bird motif being the focal point. The finish appears matte, with a slightly rough texture typical of hand-crafted ceramics. The vase is hollow and likely intended for decorative use, possibly to hold dried flowers or as a standalone art piece.
description: A tall, terracotta vase with a narrow neck and bulbous body, decorated with black painted bird and geometric motifs. The surface is matte and slightly rough, with intricate traditional patterns.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main bird motif with detailed wings and spiral patterns, as well as the neck's horizontal bands.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the continuation of the bird's wing and geometric patterns, with less detail visible compared to the front.
description_view_2: The back view displays the rear portion of the bird motif and more abstract geometric designs, with the neck's bands still visible.
description_view_3: This view focuses on the opposite side of the bird motif, showing more of the spiral and line patterns, with less of the bird's head visible.
